Limit of 3 tonnes gross vehicle and plant weight limit applicable to all concourse and platform areas. Requests for dispensation up to a higher limit must be accompanied by a structural assessment and submitted to the Senior Asset Manager for consideration.

25,000-volt AC electric traction supply cables, from overhead wires, electrify all tracks at Fenchurch Street Station. All works undertaken on the station must be undertaken in accordance with the Network Rail instructions concerning personal safety on electrified lines

There are no 3rd or 4th rail electric conductor rails at this station. Note: Portable metal steps and ladders <electrical conductors> are specially banned from Fenchurch Street Station in order to avoid the danger of contact with traction supply equipment.

Limit of 7 tonnes gross vehicle and plant weight limit applicable from the access road Smallbrook Queensways. Requests for dispensation up to a higher limit must be accompanied by a structural assessment and submitted to the Senior Asset Manager for consideration.

Limit of 2.5 tonnes gross vehicle and plant weight limit applicable to this short stay car park. Requests for dispensation up to a higher limit must be accompanied by a structural assessment and submitted to the Senior Asset Manager for consideration.

All tracks have overhead 25,000-volt AC electric traction supply cables. All works undertaken on the station must be undertaken in accordance with the Network Rail instructions concerning personal safety on electrified line.

There are numerous power cable runs of both high and low voltage above and below ground level throughout the station. Each contractor will be responsible for the scanning of the work site in order to locate the exact location of the underground power cable
Limited records of the drainage layout are held by the Senior Asset Manager and the Facility Delivery Manager. Contractors should note that drainage exists beneath platforms.

Water mains and meter located in front of retail outlets at main entrance to station. Limited records of water supply pipelines are held by the Senior Asset Manager and the Facility Delivery Manager. Contractors should note that these would be present to most areas of the station, including platforms.

Hot water is distributed and supplied to the "Raven Bar" and "Upper Crust" by means of a network of pipes many of which are lagged. Other locations have their own individual electric hot water heaters.

Records of gas supply pipelines are held by the Senior Asset Manager and the Facility Delivery Manager. All mains gas supply pipes up to and including the meter are the responsibility of National Grid. In case of emergency the station has contact details. Natural gas is non-toxic, but large concentrations could cause suffocation. It is highly flammable and leakage from a pipeline could result in an explosive mixture with air. Noise of escaping gas could be sufficient to cause damage to hearing and ear protection must be worn. All persons must be kept 100 metres from the damaged pipeline.
Asbestos hazards please see ARMS for specific details

The Boiler Room contains Gas, Hot/Cold Water and Electricity services, which present potential hazards of explosion, asphyxiation, scolding and electrocution. It has a high ceiling and the boilers produce significant levels of noise and may cause a high Personal Dose if work was carried out over a protracted period. Pipes cross the walking access routes at approximately 1.8m resulting in a potential for head injuries.
High Voltage Switch Rooms has not been accessed but is reported to constitute a Limited Access area and presents hazards relating to electrocution.

Medium Voltage Switch Room has not been accessed and presents hazards relating to electrocution. In addition there is a caged area containing transformer equipment which also constitutes hazards relating to electrocution.
A Gas cupboard is situated near the road access. All gas services constitute potential explosion and asphyxiation hazards.

Located at platforms. Each platform has a Low Voltage Switch Rooms. These rooms have Limited Access and extensive electrical equipment resulting in electrocution hazards. There is also cabling at height, which could lead to hazards from working at height and falling objects.

Records exist of drainage, electrical and water supply pipelines held in Station Reception, and with Senior Asset Manager and the Facility Delivery Manager. Contractors should note that these would be present to most areas of the station, including platforms.
Special precautions apply in all relay rooms and non-renewed locations.

There are many areas above rooms located throughout Fenchurch Street Station. Typical rooms contain obvious common electrical hazards in the form of high and low voltage electrical cables, associated switchgear, control panels and cabinets. Fire alarm system control panels are often also present. There are often no warning signs on entry doors indicating equipment voltages but access is restricted to authorised personnel only.

St Catherine's Row. Located within the room is a large heating ducting below head height which winds its way from the two heating boilers into a room with a shaft and veritical riser. Within this shaft adjacent to this ducting is a fixed metal ladder <R049>. The area this ladder leads to a room void. Consideration must be given to areas classified as Limited Access and Confined Space <The Confined Spaces Regulations 1997> must be met, please apply Clauses 1 & 2 Monitoring of access paperwork. Hazard/Risk Assessment to be carried out for activity - Results of assessment will determine control measures. Air monitoring before entry. Breathing apparatus to be available for rescue.

To the right on entering the Boiler Room is the Gas Meter Room <R051>. This contains the main gas control valve and the gas meter. Within this room the hazards are gas related e.g. leaks etc. Beyond the entrance to the Boiler Room is a stairway leading down into the main level of the Boiler Room, containing the heating boilers and related pumps and electrical distribution board. It is not known if any of the lagging contains asbestos.Adjoining the Boiler Room is Room R048 which is the Main Switch Room containing the main distribution board. There is a fire exit from the Boiler Room into this Switch Room and another from the switch Room into the Nat West basement and the door facing leads to St Catherine's Row.

Coopers Row under Arch K, on entering the short corridor is a raised floor level where the light switch is located. The fixed metal ladder within the shaft to a point approximately 20 feet above. Main cables and pipework in this area which rise against the wall of the shaft. The fixed metal ladder has no safety hoops. At the bottom of this ladder is a light weight inspection cover giving access to a basement area. There are no access steps or ladder. Consideration must be given to areas classified as Limited Access and Confined Space <The Confined Spaces Regulations 1997> must be met, please apply Clauses 1 & 2 Monitoring of access paperwork. Hazard/Risk Assessment to be carried out for activity - Results of assessment will determine control measures. Air monitoring before entry. Breathing apparatus to be available for rescue.

Air Conditioning Units located to the side of the marked walkway and behind handrails. These serve retail unit.The ventilation duct from below ground level at the end of the walkway would indicate basement services. The access for these are unknown but if found should be considered a confined space. CCTV cameras monitor the area. Any work taking place in this area must recognise the need to keep the walkway clear for use.Consideration must be given to areas classified as Confined Space <The Confined Spaces Regulations 1997> must be met, please apply Clauses 2 Monitoring of access paperwork. Hazard/Risk Assessment to be carried out for activity - Results of assessment will determine control measures. Air monitoring before entry. Breathing apparatus to be available for rescue

The room leads in from French Ordinary Court bin area. Air conditioning ducting near the entrance is at head level and pipework and cable runs on the walls. There is an electrical distribution board in one corner and a caged meter in another. The Bin Room has a rubbish chute from the ceiling and normally a rubbish bin is positioned below it. If it is necessary to move the bin then the rubbish chute shield should be placed over the chute opening to prevent rubbish falling through. This area has an obvious attraction for vermin and persons working in this area should be aware of Weil's disease as well as the more obvious biological, electrical and head bumping hazards.

Located at Tower exit at Coopers Row, within the fire exit of No.1 America Square. The shaft is very narrow and houses the shutter mechanism and the recess above is where the motor is located. Access to this area is by agreement with the occupiers of No.1 America Square and any work activity taking place must not be in conflict with that of the occupants. There are other obvious risks of entanglement and falls.

The door from the Towerhill entrance gives access to a short corridor and contains the light switches for the public area and doors to two staff toilets and switch room. The switch room contains a distribution board, water meter and a telephone signal enhancer. The hazards in this area are electricity related and biological.

The void behind plasma screeners at Tower Hill exit. This is a narrow unlit space between a concrete wall and a false wall. This is a limited access with extremely limited space for manoeuvre with numerous cable runs, pipework, false wall space bars and fire hose. The hazards in this area are electricity related. Consideration must be given to areas classified as Limited Access please apply Clauses 1 Monitoring of access paperwork. Hazard/Risk Assessment to be carried out for activity - Results of assessment will determine control measures.
This is a small outlet with a switchboard in a small storeroom. The hazards are electricity related.

Accessed from the subway between the platforms & from the Towerhill entrance stairway.Within the room a small unused cupboard below the stairway has low headroom. At the opposite end of the Locker Room is a door that is kept locked. It leads to a room that is only partially lit and has a floor with a number of different levels containing narrow access corridors for services. Beyond this there is low headroom and an unlit area. Another locked door gives access to cable runs and pipework from a very narrow ledge. The access door keys are kept in a safe deposit box in the Station Manager's Office. Consideration must be given to areas classified as Limited Access please apply Clauses 1 Monitoring of access paperwork. Hazard/Risk Assessment to be carried out for activity - Results of assessment will determine control measures.

Located to the left on entering the station from the main entrance, Cistern Corridor. There are electrical, hazardous substances and biological hazards present. The cisterns and toilet apparatus is located in a narrow void accessed by removing the wall panels. In the corridor between the Ladies Toilets and the Attendants Room there is a ceiling hatch. This void has an uneven floor that has not been boarded. It is unlit and there are small cable runs. Consideration must be given to areas classified as Limited Access and Confined Space <The Confined Spaces Regulations 1997> must be met, please apply Clauses 1 & 2 Monitoring of access paperwork. Hazard/Risk Assessment to be carried out for activity - Results of assessment will determine control measures. Air monitoring before entry. Breathing apparatus to be available for rescue.
This is accessed from the corridor in the Toilet Attendants Rooms. It contains an Isolator Switch Board and associated cable and ducting runs at ceiling level to retail unit.

Located off the Toilet Attendants corridor. The light in the access corridor is approximately 6 foot above floor level with narrow access metal stair beyond this to the motor room. Distribution boards, cabling and pipework are either against the walls or at ceiling level. The two escalator motors are at either side of the room, displaying signs stating the mandatory use of ear protection and head protection.The motors and switchboard are on the top landing area. Activity carried out down the stairway and below the escalators would offer a considerable height restriction. Consideration must be given to areas classified as Limited Access and Confined Space <The Confined Spaces Regulations 1997> must be met, please apply Clauses 1 & 2 Monitoring of access paperwork. Hazard/Risk Assessment to be carried out for activity - Results of assessment will determine control measures. Air monitoring before entry. Breathing apparatus to be available for rescue.

Situated to the left when entering from the main entrance at Fenchurch Place. Redundant waste pipes from Nat West building above and small narrow switch room may be present. There are main cable runs and lagged pipework high above and also in a shaft, accessed through the removed blocks in a dividing wall. To enter this shaft confined space must be considered. There is a drop to the floor level below <distance unknown>. Consideration must be given to areas classified as Limited Access and Confined Space <The Confined Spaces Regulations 1997> must be met, please apply Clauses 1 & 2 Monitoring of access paperwork. Hazard/Risk Assessment to be carried out for activity - Results of assessment will determine control measures. Air monitoring before entry. Breathing apparatus to be available for rescue.

Located on the left of the main entrance at Fenchurch Place. This room contains the distribution switchboards for the lift and shop unit and also the lift motor. The cable runs are wall mounted above head height.

Various service equipment located at Fenchurch Place, Station entrance by retail outlets. A switch room within the Superdrug premises which houses a telephone enhancer, a distribution switch board and electricity meter for "FENNS", the sandwich bar adjacent to Superdrug. There is an inspection cover on the pavement. It is not known which services it contains or how deep it is.
There is no fixed access to the canopy and all roof areas should be considered as potentially fragile.

Within the leased premises, offices, storerooms etc, there is a suspended ceiling with a void above containing various services. These can be accessed from the hatch in the Telecoms Switch room. This area has low headroom. Consideration must be given to areas classified as Limited Access and Confined Space <The Confined Spaces Regulations 1997> must be met, please apply Clauses 1 & 2 Monitoring of access paperwork. Hazard/Risk Assessment to be carried out for activity - Results of assessment will determine control measures. Air monitoring before entry. Breathing apparatus to be available for rescue.

There are two platforms, each with voids below and columns either supporting the buildings above or the canopies. There are a number of inspection covers giving access to the area below the platforms. This is thought to be a shallow space providing drainage pipework and cable runs likely to be electrical and biological hazards. Consideration must be given to areas classified as Limited Access and Confined Space <The Confined Spaces Regulations 1997> must be met, please apply Clauses 1 & 2 Monitoring of access paperwork. Hazard/Risk Assessment to be carried out for activity - Results of assessment will determine control measures. Air monitoring before entry. Breathing apparatus to be available for rescue.

The Cleaning compound is located off the main concourse on the opposite side from the Customer Service Office. The area stores cleaning materials and equipment and for the recharging of the floor cleaning equipment. There is also a sink, hot water heater and cleaning operative's lockers. Three of the walls are partition walls. Within this compound is an access door to the BT Police CCTV camera switch room.

Located adjacent to the Cleaning Compound and opposite the Customer Service Office. This is a room with partition walls on two sides and contains a large chilling unit and associated motors which is not in use. The switch board for the units is positioned above the motors which makes them difficult to access. The distribution board for "Upper Crust", the "Raven Bar" and the Ticket Machine is located here and the cabling, ducting and piping is attached to the walls. The room is also used for the storage of stepladders and trolleys.

Located on the main concourse adjacent to the Customer Service Office. This is a shelved store room which is built of partitions with pigeon spikes on the top edges. The room contains paper products and cleaning materials labelled with hazard warnings.

Located adjacent to the Customer Service Office. The access door leads to a small corridor with a hatch in the ceiling providing access to the ceiling void. The switch room itself contains various telecoms and clock apparatus. This room also has a hatch in the ceiling, which gives access to the services in the ceiling void above the Customer Service Office, W H Smith and at least part of the concourse. There is extremely limited headroom, the floor is uneven with numerous cables and pipework at floor level. Consideration must be given to areas classified as Limited Access please apply Clauses 1 Monitoring of access paperwork. Hazard/Risk Assessment to be carried out for activity - Results of assessment will determine control measures.

In the entrance corridor there are enclosed downpipes within clad walls servicing NatWest. There is a switch room at the end of the corridor containing a distribution board and meters. The main fire alarm panel is located at the Customer Information Point.
Notification of route crime hotspot with this area subject to repeated incidents of trespass and graffiti
Notification of route crime hotspot with this area subject to repeated incidents of trespass and graffiti

Local Name: PUDDING MILL LANE ATFS, BARBERS ROAD
Free Text: PUDDING MILL LANE AUTOTRANSFORMER FEEDER STATION <PML ATFS> PROVIDES 25KV ELECTRICAL POWER TO BOTH THE CROSSRAIL CENTRAL TUNNELS AND THE GREAT EASTERN MAIN LINE <GEML>. THE TRACTION EARTH RETURN SYSTEM FROM THE CENTRAL TUNNELS IS TERMINATED ON THE SAME RETURN CURRENT BUS BAR <RCBB> AS THE TRACTION EARTH RETURN FROM THE GEML. TO WORK ON THE RCBB, BOTH THE CENTRAL TUNNELS AND GEML REQUIRE ISOLATION. THIS IS DOCUMENTED IN THE OPERATION & MAINTENANCE MANUAL, ROMFORD ELECTRICAL CONTROL ROOM INSTRUCTIONS AND A LOCAL DISTRIBUTION WORK INSTRUCTION.

Local Name: PUDDING MILL LANE ATFS, BARBERS ROAD
Free Text: PUDDING MILL LANE AUTOTRANSFORMER FEEDER STATION <PML ATFS> HAS 2NO. HIGH VOLTAGE CABLE CHAMBERS <4MX4MX4M APPROX>. 1NO. CHAMBER IS IN THE ADJACENT RAIL FOR LONDON <RFL> SITE. BOTH WILL COME UNDER NETWORK RAIL MAINTENANCE RESPONSIBILITY. BOTH CHAMBERS ARE USUALLY FULL OF WATER <AS BELOW THE WATER TABLE>. ACCESS TO EACH REQUIRES THEM TO BE FIRST PUMPED DRY OVER 24 HOURS. DURING THIS TIME PART OF THE CHAMBER COVER WILL BE OPEN. THERE IS RESIDUAL RISK AT SITE WITH THESE CONFINED SPACES AND DEEP WATER. ACCESS TO THESE CHAMBERS IS REQUIRED EVERY 12 MONTHS TO VISUALLY INSPECT THE HV CABLES BY NETWORK RAIL MAINTAINER.

PUDDING MILL LANE AUTOTRANSFORMER FEEDER STATION <PM ATFS> PROVIDES 25KV ELECTRICAL POWER TO BOTH THE CROSSRAIL CENTRAL TUNNELS AND THE GREAT EASTERN MAIN LINE <GEML>. THE SWITCHGEAR TO THE CENTRAL TUNNELS ARE OPERATED BY ATC/CROSSRAIL LTD. SOON TO BE RFL AT ROMFORD CONTROL CENTRE <RCC>; WHILST THE SWITCHGEAR FEEDING THE GEML AND THE SWITCHGEAR BETWEEN NATIONAL GRID AND PML ATFS ARE OPERATED BY NETWORK RAIL AT ROMFORD ELECTRICAL CONTROL ROOM <ECR> IN THE RCC. THE RFL SWITCHGEAR IS FENCED OFF FROM THE REMAINDER OF THE NETWORK RAIL PML ATFS COMPOUND, BUT THERE REMAINS THE POSSIBILITY THAT RFL COULD OPERATE THEIR SWITCHGEAR WHILST NETWORK RAIL <NR> MAINTAINERS ARE AT PML ATFS MAINTAINING/OPERATING THE NR PORTION OF THE SITE. THERE ARE ELECTRICAL CONTROL ROOM INSTRUCTIONS <ECRI> AND WORK INSTRUCTION <WI> PROCEDURES TO CONTROL ACCESS AND WAYS OF WORKING, BUT OPERATIVES ENTERING PML ATFS SHOULD BE AWARE AND KEEP AWAY FROM THE RFL SWITCHGEAR.
